name: Town Of Cheektowaga
description_short: Town of Cheektowaga Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative with over 1,500 members and $37M in assets, offering checking, savings, loans, and mortgage services.
description_long:
Town of Cheektowaga Federal Credit Union has served its community for 44 years, building membership and assets through shareholder loyalty and engagement. The credit union now boasts over 1,500 members and more than $37 million in assets, reflecting steady growth and community trust. As a federally-chartered credit union (NCUA-insured), it operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned model distinct from traditional banks.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including free checking accounts with no monthly fees, free checks, free ATM/debit cards, and access to 30,000 ATMs nationwide. Members can open various savings accounts tailored to different needs and timelines, apply for loans at competitive rates, and access mortgage services through a partnership with CUMA (Credit Union Mortgage Association). Additional services include bill pay, Visa credit and debit cards, student loans through Sallie Mae, online banking, and loan/savings calculators.

What distinguishes Town of Cheektowaga FCU is its local community focus and emphasis on member relationships built over four decades. The credit union explicitly markets the membership experience and values accumulated shareholder loyalty. Their mortgage education resources and partnership with CUMA provide structured guidance for home financing decisions. The institution's smaller size compared to large national banks allows for personalized service while maintaining broad ATM access.

As a small, local credit union, Town of Cheektowaga FCU serves members primarily in its geographic area and may have limited digital sophistication compared to larger financial institutions. While they offer competitive products, the website provides minimal detail on specific rates, APRs, or loan terms. Membership eligibility requirements are not explicitly stated on the website, though credit union membership is typically restricted to specific communities or employee groups.
services: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees
Savings accounts (multiple types for different needs)
Mortgage loans and refinancing (through CUMA partnership)
Free ATM/debit cards with access to 30,000 ATMs
Visa credit cards and Visa debit cards
pros: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees or check reorder costs
Access to 30,000 ATMs nationwide despite being a small local credit union
Free online bill pay and ATM/debit card services included
Established 44-year history with $37M+ in assets and 1,500+ members
Mortgage services partnership with CUMA including educational resources
Competitive loan rates across multiple product types
cons: Website does not display specific interest rates, APRs, or loan terms
No clear information about membership eligibility requirements or restrictions
Limited online functionality based on website content (basic online banking mentioned)
Smaller institution may have fewer service options compared to larger credit unions
No information provided about customer service availability or response times
best_for: Employees or residents of the Town of Cheektowaga area seeking local financial services
Members prioritizing free checking and low-cost banking with ATM access
Homebuyers interested in mortgage financing with educational guidance
diagnosis: Town of Cheektowaga FCU is best for local community members and employees seeking free or low-cost checking and basic lending services from an established, member-owned institution. The main caveat is limited transparency on rates and terms on their website, requiring direct contact to compare specific loan offerings and determine membership eligibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Town of Cheektowaga — NCUA-insured credit union in CHEEKTOWAGA, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
